concerns.analysis: The source document contains critical, full-page map exhibits on pages 12, 13, and 14 (Vicinity Exhibit, USGS Topographic Exhibit, and Site Exhibit). In the HTML output, these maps fail to render as images and are instead completely replaced by literal text placeholders enclosed in brackets (e.g., '[Image: A vicinity map...]'). Because these visual maps are highly meaningful to the environmental assessment and are missing their corresponding elements in the output, this is a critical defec
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Pages 12, 13, and 14 contain full-page map exhibits detailing the project vicinity, topography, and site layout.
concerns.output_quote:
[Image: A vicinity map for the Cumberland County/Cliffdale Landfill displaying the Project Area... 0 to 4,000 feet.]

(appears without any corresponding tag)

concerns.description: Table split across page break(s): 2 table(s) with repeated headers across consecutive pages
concerns.analysis:
split_table_count=2. Examples: pages 16-17: 3 cols (s&me, site photographs
cliffdale landfill
fayetteville, north carolina, s&me project no. 23050459...); pages 23-24: 2 cols (name, status...). Tables spanning multiple pages were extracted as separate table elements with duplicated header rows. The HTML output will show multiple tables instead of one continuous table — a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: Full-page image alongside extracted text (1 page(s))
concerns.analysis: suspect_pages=1. A scanned/screenshot page image duplicates content already present as text elements.

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 16 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=16, examples=['http://www.fws.gov', 'mailto:Raleigh@fws.gov', 'http://www.fws.gov/']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Header/footer elements retained in output (40): Version 5/22/2023; Applicant; Page 2
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=40, pages_affected=19/29. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML — the renderer failed to strip it. This is a structural accessibility issue.

concerns.description: 1 bare URL(s) in text not converted to hyperlinks: (fws.gov
concerns.analysis: bare_url_count=1. URLs appear as plain text instead of clickable links. Per NCDIT criteria, link text not wrapped in href is a major defect.

concerns.description: Table cells contain 10 unlinked URL(s)/email(s) that should be hyperlinks
concerns.analysis: bare_urls_in_tables=10. Examples: p23: https://ecos.fws.gov/ecp/species/10515; p23: https://ecos.fws.gov/ecp/species/7614; p23: https://ecos.fws.gov/ecp/species/776. URLs and emails in table cells should be clickable links.

concerns.description: Footnote(s) converted to bulleted list: 1 unordered list(s) after table(s) appear to be footnotes
concerns.analysis: footnote_as_list_count=1. Examples: 1 item(s): "NOTE: If the proposed project intersects with a co". Table footnotes (often marked with *, †, ‡) were converted to <ul><li> elements instead of paragraphs. This changes the semantic meaning — screen readers announce them as list items rather than explanatory notes.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 1 table(s) have a data row marked as header: "S&ME"
concerns.analysis: data_in_th=0, datarow_as_header=1. Tables use <th> for data values (misrepresenting data as headers) or have a data row mismarked as a header row. Screen readers rely on <th> to announce column/row context.

concerns.description: Form elements detected: 3 form(s) with 7 field(s) — forms lose functionality in HTML
concerns.analysis: form_count=3, total_fields=7. PDF form fields (checkboxes, text inputs, signatures) cannot be faithfully rendered as static HTML.

The source PDF uses continuous underlining to emphasize the 'Status' and 'Biological Determination' lines for each species evaluated in the report (Pages 5-8). In the extraction JSON, these lines are extracted as plain text paragraphs without any underline formatting, resulting in a genuine loss of visual emphasis.
concerns.source_pdf_quote:
Status: Federally-Proposed Endangered
Biological Determination: No consultation necessary (Both lines are underlined in the source PDF)
concerns.output_quote: "text": "Status: Federally-Proposed Endangered\nBiological Determination: No consultation necessary"

concerns.analysis: Short text with footer-like pattern appears on 2 pages
Short text with footer-like pattern appears on 2 pages
